details: Compound 8b '2-(2-(Benzyloxy)-5-chloro-4-methylphenyl)-2-oxoethyl acetate' in paper 113 (DOI 10.1021/acsomega.6c01037) reports 'mp 90-92 °C' exactly as recorded.
Compound 3d full IUPAC name and 'mp 218°C (decomp.)' confirmed verbatim in paper 183 (DOI 10.1134/S1070428022030101).
Compound 4b '3-(Cyclohexylamino)-2-(4-nitrophenyl)dibenzo[b,f]pyrrolo[1,2-d][1,4]oxazepine-1-carbonitrile' with 'mp 242-245 °C' confirmed in norouzi_2024 (DOI 10.3762/bjoc.20.241).
Compound 8b '2-(4-Methoxybenzenesulfonyl)-1,3,5-trimethylbenzene' with 'mp 132-133 °C' confirmed in Zhou 2013 (DOI 10.1021/jm3014162).
Table 1 of Krossing 2011 (DOI 10.1002/cphc.201100522) row '[EMIm][OTos] 53.3 55.5 52.6 55.1 49.8' confirms 55.5 as Tp (peak maximum) first cycle = DSC_peak.
Compound 2f '3-(2-furanyl)-6-(3-chlorophenyl)-1,2,4-triazolo[3,4-b]-1,3,4-thiadiazole' with 'M.p.207-209°C' confirmed in paper 090 (DOI 10.3390/70800681).
Table 2 of paper 017 (DOI 10.3390/molecules28227523) lists [Glu(OPent)2][HCl] Tm = 69.24°C; matches row.
Compound 3b '7,7-dichloro-2-oxabicyclo[4.1.0]heptane' and Table 1 row b 'B.P. 45-47/133' = bp 45-47°C at 133 Pa confirmed in paper 089 (DOI 10.3390/80800608).
Compound 14 with full IUPAC name and 'mp 46-49 °C' confirmed in paper 116 (DOI 10.3390/molecules31050796).
Compound 5m with full IUPAC name and 'mp: 200-202 °C' confirmed in paper 169 (DOI 10.1021/acsbiomedchemau.4c00060).
Compound 5 '6-Phenylamino-4-benzalaminothieno[3,2-d]pyrimidin-7-carbonitrile' with 'm.p. 243 oC' confirmed in paper 103 (DOI 10.3390/71000756).

Paper rogers_2021 (DOI 10.3390/molecules26134034) describes 27.5°C explicitly as a glass-transition point: 'Both are isotropic ILs above their glass transition point. [Na][C8E8c] is liquid above 27.5 °C'. Glass transition is not among the six allowed properties (melting_point, boiling_point, DSC_onset, DSC_peak, decomposition, sublimation). The extraction notes acknowledge this ambiguity but property=melting_point doesn't match the paper's stated phenomenon.

Table 1 row 4 of fini_2013 (DOI 10.3390/pharmaceutics2020136) shows TRIS-methylolaminomethane diclofenac with Tpeak = 209°C; paper prose also refers to this as melting point ('TRIS a m. p. higher than 209 ºC (Table 1)').
Stigmasterol with 'm.p. 155-156 °C' confirmed in paper 155 (DOI 10.3390/molecules15106733).
16-Oxoaspidospermidine (5b) with 'm.p. 108-112 °C' confirmed in paper 096 (DOI 10.3390/61000803).
3-Hydroxy-2-methyl-3,3-diphenylpropanoic acid 'Melting point: 180 °C' confirmed in paper 055 (PMC3685236).
Tyrindoleninone 'red needles, mp 109.5 °C' confirmed in paper 141 (DOI 10.3390/60900736); compiled from cited ref [112] but still an experimental observation, so meas_calc=measured is correct.
Compound 5a '8-Hydrazino-2,4-dimethyl-2H-1,2,3-benzothiadiazine 1,1-dioxide' with 'Mp 160.5-161.5 °C (EtOH)' confirmed in pusztai_2025 (DOI 10.3762/bjoc.21.169).
Table 1 of paper 139 (DOI 10.3390/61100881) row '3-Me B 71 153-55 154 [22]' confirms 1-(Diacetoxyiodo)-3-methylbenzene experimental mp 153-55°C; midpoint 154 is correct.

Ethyl 2-benzamido-2-(p-tolylamino)acetate (3') experimental section reports 'Yield 80%; Solid, m.p. 164-166 °C' in paper 034 (DOI 10.3390/molecules15129354). Table 1 entry 3 with R=Et also shows 164-166. Quote in row contains MS data prepended but contains the value and compound name.
Compound 6b 'Methyl-2-[2-(5-benzyl-4-phenyl-4H-[1,2,4]triazol-3-thio)-acetylamino]-acetate' with 'mp 114 °C' confirmed in paper 035 (DOI 10.3390/molecules15106759).
Lee 2021 (DOI 10.3390/molecules26071983) form I DSC onset at 267.5°C is tied to CK decomposition in the prose; property=decomposition is supported.
Dearden 2003 (DOI 10.1897/01-363) Table 2 'Styrene 145.2 146.7 ...' — 145.2 is b.p.(exp.) column, matching row's value and meas_calc=measured.
Table 1 row 9 of fini_2013 (DOI 10.3390/pharmaceutics2020136) 'Methyl-diethanolamine MeDEA 248 415.31 102 ...' shows Tpeak=102°C; paper prose also calls 102°C the melting point of MeDEA salt.
Table 1 of paper 139 (DOI 10.3390/61100881) row '2-Me C 64 140-42 140-142 [21]' confirms 1-(Diacetoxyiodo)-2-methylbenzene experimental mp 140-42°C; midpoint 141 is correct.
